service.description: Mint a per-client API key for the public GraphQL tier. SECURITY: generates a cryptographically-random key (SecureRandom, 256 bits, URL-safe base64, prefixed "dgql_"), stores ONLY its SHA-256 hex digest, and returns the RAW key exactly ONCE in the `apiKey` out-param. The raw key is NOT recoverable afterwards — capture it now or re-mint. Admin-only key minting: this does NOT require tenant membership, so a super-admin can mint keys for any tenant. companyUserGroupId is the tenant every read made with this key is fixed to.

parameter.name: companyUserGroupId
parameter.description: The tenant this key is scoped to.

parameter.name: apiKey
parameter.description: The RAW key, shown ONCE and not recoverable. Deliver securely to the client.

service.description: Internal: validate a presented raw API key fail-closed and return its fixed tenant + profile. SECURITY: hashes the key (SHA-256) and looks it up BY HASH (no raw compare, no substring/like match) — this also avoids timing leaks on the raw key. A blank/missing key, no matching row, enabled != 'Y', or a passed expireDate all resolve as invalid. On any failure `valid` is false and the error message is the generic "invalid API key" (the specific failing check is never leaked). On success returns companyUserGroupId/profileId/apiKeyId/ clientName and best-effort updates lastUsedDate.
